title: The Antique Specialist
description: Pick a modern object, then exclusively refer to it by its "archaic" name. (e.g., 'pocket telegraph' for phone, 'davenport' for sofa)

title: The Unearned Expert
description: Pick a boring topic (e.g., history of cardboard) and pretend to be an obsessive enthusiast.

title: The Parental Reflex
description: Pick a target, then sincerely treat that person like a child. (e.g., ask 'Did you wash your hands?' or 'Do you need a bathroom break?)

title: The Slow Clapper
description: Start a slow, rhythmic clap after someone finishes a sentence, a drink, etc.

title: The Sommelier of Water
description: Swirl your water glass, sniff it deeply, and describe its "earthy notes."

title: The Echo
description: Repeat the last three words of someone else's sentence under your breath.

title: The Safety Inspector
description: Before sitting down, pick up the chair and inspect the bottom for a "safety certification" mark.

title: The Corporate Pivot
description: Use at least five corporate buzzwords (e.g., "synergy", "touch base", "low-hanging fruit") in a single story.

title: The Mirror
description: Pick a target. Every time they take a drink, you must immediately take a drink (real or invisible).

title: The Invisible Barrier
description: Act as if there is an invisible wall between two pieces of furniture, refusing to walk between them.

title: The One-Minute Delay
description: Answer a direct question exactly 60 seconds after it was asked.

title: The Thumb War
description: Attempt to initiate a thumb war with someone during a serious or quiet moment.

title: The Nickname Generator
description: Give two different people nicknames based on tropical fruits and only use those names for the round.

title: The Yoga Master
description: Incorporate one obvious yoga pose (like "Tree Pose") into a standing conversation.

title: The Sneeze Maestro
description: Sneeze three times, making each sneeze sound increasingly like a different musical note.

title: The Imaginary Buddy
description: Reference a mutual "friend" named Bartholomew and act surprised when others say they don't know him.

title: The Sound Effect
description: Make a distinct "whoosh" or "boop" sound every time you sit down or stand up.

title: The Air Quotes
description: Use "air quotes" around words that definitely don't require them (e.g., 'I'm going to the "kitchen" to get some "water"').

title: The Formal Bow
description: Give a deep, Victorian-style bow to anyone who performs a small favor for you (e.g., passing a snack.)

title: The Flavor Profile
description: Ask someone what 'color' their food or drink tastes like, then disagree with their answer.

title: The Shadow Boxer
description: After making a point in a conversation, perform three quick, silent punches into the air.

title: The Time Traveler
description: Refer to a common modern object (like a microwave or TV) as 'primitive technology' or 'a classic relic.'

title: The Background Music
description: Hum a suspenseful, movie-trailer style theme song while another player is telling a story.

title: The Networker
description: Introduce a player to someone they already know as if they have never met.

title: The Acronym Enthusiast
description: Invent an acronym for a common object (e.g., 'Pass me the B.O.S.' for Bottle of Soda) and refuse to explain it.

title: The Ghost Hunter
description: Suddenly stop, look at a corner of the room, and ask if anyone else "felt that cold spot."

title: The Professional Handshaker
description: Shake someone's hand for a full 10 seconds while maintaining intense eye contact.

title: The Third Person
description: Refer to yourself in the third person for the duration of a single story or explanation.

title: The Prop Master
description: Pick up a kitchen utensil (like a whisk or spatula) and carry it with you as a "talking stick".

title: The Weather Reporter
description: Provide a detailed 5-day weather forecast for a city that is at least 1,000 miles away.

title: The Silent Librarian
description: Shush the entire room loudly when the conversation is already at a reasonable volume.

title: The Pocket Sand
description: Pretend to throw "invisible sand" at someone to "distract them" while you walk away from a conversation.

title: The Fashion Police
description: Give a player a genuine, high-energy compliment on their "brave and avant-garde" choice of clothing.

title: The Slow Motion
description: Perform one entire task (like pouring a drink or putting on a jacket) in exaggerated slow motion.

title: The Fortune Teller
description: Predict a very mundane event (e.g., "Someone is going to walk through that door") and celebrate if it happens.

title: The Director
description: Tell two people to "reset the scene" and ask them to repeat their last interaction.

title: The Compliment Sandwich
description: Give someone a compliment, followed by a confusingly specific insult, followed by another compliment.

title: The Historical Footnote
description: Correct someone on a completely fabricated "historical fact" about the house or building you are currently in.

title: The Animal Kingdom
description: Compare three different players to specific breeds of dogs and explain your reasoning in detail.

title: The Mime
description: Act as if you are stuck in an invisible box, then immediately stop and act normal.

title: The Unsolicited Advice
description: Give someone "expert" advice on a better way to hold their phone or drink for "maximum ergonomic efficiency."

title: The Missing Item
description: Ask the group if anyone has seen your "emotional support stapler" (or another non-existent weird object).

title: The Aggressive Tipper
description: Try to hand someone a coin, bill, or token as a "tip" for a very small favor, like passing the salt.

title: The Looming Presence
description: Stand slightly too close to a player (within their personal bubble), then walk away without a word.

title: The Inconspicuous Re-aligner
description: Identify a set of objects (coasters, chairs, pens) and slowly, over several minutes, align them perfectly parallel to one another.

title: The Culinary Critic
description: After taking a bite of any food, give a 30-second review as if you are a judge on a high-stakes cooking show.

title: The Human Compass
description: When you move to a different room, announce which cardinal direction (North, South, etc.) you are now facing.

title: The 'Pinky Out' Protocol
description: Drink with your pinky finger dramatically extended.

title: The Invisible Hat
description: When you enter or leave a conversation, perform a "tip of the hat" gesture to the other person, despite wearing no hat.

title: The Standing Ovation
description: Stand up and clap enthusiastically when someone mentions they are going to get a refill or a snack.

title: The New Recruit
description: Pick a target, then exclusively refer to that person as "Captain" or "Chief".

title: The Slow-Motion High Five
description: Initiate a high five with someone, but move your hand toward theirs in extreme slow motion.

title: The Translation Error
description: Pretend to forget the common English word for a basic object (like "fork" or "door") and describe it poorly until someone else names it.

title: The Pulse Check
description: Stop mid-sentence, check your own pulse on your neck for 10 seconds with a worried expression, then continue talking.

title: The Legend
description: Whenever anyone mentions a mundane task they did today, respond with "that is absolutely legendary."

title: The Space Architect
description: Use your hands to "measure" the distance between two people talking, nodding thoughtfully as you do.

title: The Birthday Confusion
description: Sincerely wish someone "Happy Birthday." When they say it's not their birthday, apologize and say, "Oh, right, that was someone else."

title: The Thumb-Tuck
description: Keep both of your thumbs tucked into your palms and hidden inside your fists for at least ten minutes of conversation.

title: The Cinema Enthusiast
description: Insert a quote from a famous movie into a conversation, but attribute it to the wrong movie (e.g., "I'll be back - Star Wars").

title: The Invisible Cobweb
description: Suddenly swat at the air in front of your face and complain about "the ghost spiders" in this room.

title: The Indubitale Man
description: Replace every instance of the word "yes" with "indubitably."

title: The Dramatic Sigh
description: Every time you take a sip of a drink, let out a long, satisfied "Ahhhhh".

title: The Reverse Exit
description: When you leave a room, walk backward the entire way.

title: The Life Coach
description: Interrupt a story to suggest a "breath-work exercise" and try to get the speaker to take three deep breaths with you.

title: The Coin Toss
description: Flip a coin (or pretend to) before making any minor decision, like which chip to eat or where to sit.

title: The Stat Man
description: Provide a completely made-up percentage statistic to support any point you make (e.g., "Well, 64% of people prefer blue napkins").

title: The Shadow
description: Pick a target. Every time that person stands up, you stand up. When they sit, you sit. Do this whenever possible.

title: The Air Piano
description: Briefly play an "invisible piano" on the table or your knees whenever there is a lull in the conversation.

title: The Acronym Mystery
description: Start referring to the current gathering as "The O.P.P.," refusing to say what it stands for.

title: The Slow Blink
description: When someone asks you a question, blink both eyes very slowly (taking at least 3 seconds) before answering.

title: The Culinary Inventor
description: Suggest a "revolutionary" food combination (like pickles and peanut butter) and try to convince at least one person it’s a delicacy.

title: The "Wait For It"
description: Stop talking in the middle of a sentence, hold up a finger as if you're about to say something vital, wait 10 seconds, then say "nevermind."

title: The Shoe Inspector
description: Ask to see the bottom of someone's shoe to check the "tread depth" for safety reasons.

title: The Gentle Clapper
description: Clap very softly, just with your fingertips, whenever you agree with a point someone makes.

title: The Title Upgrade
description: Insist on being called by a title like "The Honorable" or "Doctor."

title: The Pocket Surprise
description: Reach into your pocket, pretend to find something invisible, and then "release it" out the nearest window or door.

title: The Mime's Drink
description: Pour an invisible drink into your actual glass, "sip" it, and comment on its vintage.

title: The Perfeft Pitch
description: Remark that you think the host's refrigerator is "humming in B-flat."

title: The Compliment Loop
description: Give the same person the exact same compliment three times, acting like it's the first time each time.

title: The Imaginary Mosquito
description: Spend a full minute trying to "catch" a non-existent bug in the air with your hands.

title: The Fact Checker
description: Wait for someone to say a number (any number), then quickly say "Actually, it's [that number plus one]."

title: The High-Stakes Toast
description: Clink your glass and propose a toast to "the inevitable passage of time."
